name: Traynor, Fred J.
motto: "She loves me. She loves me not."
description: Known as the most radical woman-hater in the institution. At present studying law in order to better understand the art of "pleading a case." We wish him success.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Thompson, Alma
description: She has determined to carry away with her a goodly share of learning, and this she has succeeded in doing. Few students have been more careful and painstaking in their work than she.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Skundberg, Agnes
motto: "A model of Innisfree sense."
description: Her motive in coming was to keep someone else from getting lonesome and to ward off all other girls.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Bosard, Andrew K.
motto: "Such depth of brow; a brainy man is he."
clubs: Slide trombone dispenser of harmonies, University Battalion Band
description: This Pennsylvania Dutchman found that Eastern culture was not thorough enough for him and came West to Grand Forks, N. D., to seek his fortune and a home in a new land.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_01.png

name: Walker, Anna B.
motto: "And all the world shall say Amen."
description: A student of rare ability; winner in local oratorical contest '02. Miss Walker excels in theatrical work.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Kristjanson, H. T.
description: Honest in purpose, persistent in endeavor, unswerving in endurance, these are the qualities which characterize Mr. Kristjanson.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Hancock, John M.
motto: "A man impartial, free from sham; His favorite palace Buckingham."
description: A noble representative of his ancestors of Revolutionary days.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Brennan, Mary R.
motto: "And still we gaze and still the wonder grows, That one small head can carry all she knows."
description: Of steadfast character, cheerful disposition. She loves to flit about at "Long" social functions.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Campbell, Thomas D.
motto: "A mighty man of muscle, the admiration of the ladies."
description: Since entering the "U" he has devoted himself unsparingly to the ladies.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_02.png

name: Lemke, Henry
major: Junior class of the Law Department
description: He moved to Cando, N. D., where he received his early education. Since his entrance at the "U" he has been a faithful and diligent student, always found where duty calls.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_03.png

name: Jennings, George
motto: "Books his favorite pastime, his knowledge not confined to texts."
description: He is one of our three North Dakota born men. According to his own account he is held in high esteem. During his summer vacations he is to be found behind the desk at the Jennings House, dispensing smiles and hospitality to the guests.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_03.png

name: Hamel, Charles D.
motto: "I am a leader among men, the cleverest man of my class. What proof? None required, I admit it."
clubs: President, U. N. D. Debating Union
Inter-collegiate Debating Team against the "U" of Manitoba '02, and "U" of South Dakota, '03
description: A youth of untiring energy and keen intellect.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_03.png

name: Chisholm, Thomas W.
motto: "Better to have loved and lost than never to have loved at all."
description: A student of excellent ability, successful alike in love and ward politics.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_03.png

name: Rysgaard, Jens M.
high school: Red Wing Seminary, at Red Wing, Minn.
clubs: President of Y. M. C. A.
description: He came to America at the age of nineteen. He possesses those sterling qualities of manhood which mark him out as a leader and benefactor of mankind.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_03.png

name: Larsen, Anna
motto: "She likes to see others happy."
clubs: Member, winning team for gold medal in 1902
description: She came to North Dakota from the "Shadow of the pines," in the old Badger state. She is one of the debaters of the Adelphi society, and was a member of the team that won the gold medal in 1902. She also was one of the originators of the now famous S. S. C.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Hillis, Arnetta
motto: "A stream that is broad and deep, peaceful in its flow."
clubs: Member, winning team for Gansel Medal '02
description: Her birthplace is in old Ontario. She stands high in all her classes; and devotes what time she has left to social recreations.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Newlander, Nanna
high school: graduate of the high school there
motto: "She is always the same, quiet and happy."
clubs: Twice elected to their team to take part in the gold medal debate
description: Old Sweden claims the honor of being the land of her birth. She came to North Dakota when quite young and has since made Grafton her home. She came to the "U" in 1901; joined the Adelphi society, and has been twice elected to their team to take part in the gold medal debate.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Odney, Stella K.
motto: "A thoughtful face, a quiet smile."
description: She came from the Gopher state; but her home is now at Thompson, N. D. She entered the "U" in 1901 and was the cause of a great, though rather short-lived, reformation in one of our popular young men.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_04.png

name: Duggan, Maude
motto: "I've some ideas of my own."
description: We are indebted to none of our neighbors for her, for she is a native of our own city of Grand Forks. She attended St. Bernard's Academy for some time before coming to the University. Unlike most girls, she says that nothing of great importance ever happened in her life.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_05.png

name: White, Robert J.
motto: "Cool, calm, and thoughtful."
description: Not returning in 1899, it was rumored that he was dead; but since then we are pleased to say that by his services to the A. D. T. in its inter-society debates, and to the University in the inter-collegiate debate against the University of Manitoba in 1902, he has shown himself to be very much alive. He is also a Y. M. C. A. boy and takes a lively interest in the welfare of that organization.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Shea, Margaret Beatrice
motto: "You can't play in my yard if you're not good to me."
hometown: on the shore of the "Raging Red"
description: From the insight which she has displayed in managing some of our elections, we conclude she intends entering upon a political career. Some of her friends, however, say that she has other plans in view.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Thordarson, Franklin
motto: "To be a force not a figure."
major: B. A. class of '01 from Gustavus Adolphus College
description: As his name indicates, he is, according to Norse mythology, a direct descendant of mighty Thor. He entered the "U" this year, not so much for the purpose of further pursuing his studies, as to have the distinction of graduating in a good-looking class. He joined the Adelphi debating society and won a place on both the inter-society and the inter-collegiate teams.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_05.png

name: Hanson, Nellie
high school: high school at Grafton, N. D.
motto: "A smiling face reflects a light heart."
description: She is a native of our own broad prairies, having always lived at Grafton, N. D. She is a graduate of the high school there. She entered the Freshman class at the "U" in 1901, but changed her course to the Normal the following year. She is one of the active members of the Adelphi society, and a leader in all social affairs at the University.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_05.png

name: McIntosh, John A.
motto: "His eloquence enchants the ear."
description: He came to North Dakota from Bruce County, Ontario, where he had been in the dairy business. As an evidence of his proficiency in that line, he shows a medal received at the World's Columbian Exposition, for the best cheese. He taught school for four years after coming to North Dakota at the end of which time he entered the Normal department of the University, where his reputation as a student has even surpassed that as a cheese maker.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Conmy, Annie C.
motto: "I'll get a joke on you if I can."
description: She came from Minneapolis to Pembina, N. D., where she finished the high school course. She entered the "U" in the fall of 1901; became an energetic member of the A. D. T. society, and afterwards of the mysterious S. S. C. She is a very brave girl, as was well demonstrated by her fearless attitude towards the "Ghost of Davis Hall."
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Dudley, May
motto: "Just to be good, that's all."
description: She was born at St. Joseph, Michigan; but for several years her home has been in Grand Forks, where she graduated from the high school in 1901. She entered the Junior Normal class at the "U" the same year; and is known in the classroom as a conscientious worker.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Ueland, Anna
motto: "To do with my might what my hands find to do."
clubs: leader in athletics among the young ladies
description: She lives at Edgeley, N. D.; but her birthplace was at Calender, Iowa. She entered the "U" and took up preparatory work in 1897, but was obliged to remain out of school for several years. She finally concluded that there was no place like the old "U," and came back to us.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Hanson, Alice G.
motto: "In her heart is the spirit of helpfulness."
description: She is a native of the Gopher State; but for several years, our own city of Grand Forks has been her home. She graduated from the high school there, and entered the Normal department at the University in 1901. She has always distinguished herself in the classroom: is an enthusiastic worker in the A. D. T. literary society and secretary of the class.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_06.png

name: Crowley, Margaret
motto: "I don't find it hard to look pleasant."
description: She came to us just this year from the Ellendale Manual Training School, where she graduated in 1902. She is a member of the A. D. T., and says that her life work is undecided, which is welcome news to several of our young men.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Owen, Edith
motto: "A little girl, but, O, MY!"
description: She numbers among her progenitors those who were famous in Celtic song and story in ancient Wales. She was born at Lime Springs, Iowa, but for some years has lived at Ellendale, N. D., where she completed the High School course and took one year's work in the Manual Training College. She entered the "U" at the beginning of this year; joined the A. D. T. Society, where she soon won distinction in a literary line.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Anderson, Jessie M.
motto: "I've a taste for the sweetness of philosophy."
description: Her home is in East Grand Forks, Minnesota. She graduated from the Grand Forks High School in 1901 and entered the University the same fall. During the last year she has manifested considerable interest in Hamline University, and we fear if she ever decides to continue her studies, we will lose her.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Brown, Marguerite
motto: "A force is none the less active because it is silent."
description: She was born in East Grand Forks, Minn., where she has spent all her life, proving that a good thing can come even from that city. She graduated from the Grand Forks High School in 1901, and entered the Junior Normal class the same year, in which she has always been held as a valuable member.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_07.png

name: Mackay, James Scott
motto: "A man of energy and purpose."
description: He lived there until he was a young man, finishing the high school work and spending some time at a technical school in Glasgow. He has been a sailor, a plumber, a school teacher, a book agent, a student, and a variety of other things, and was equally good at all.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Wehe, Blanche H.
motto: "A tall and stately form."
description: Her home for several years was in Nelson county, N. D.; but is now in Grand Forks. She took her preparatory course at the University. She has always been a faithful worker and diligent student.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Wehe, Sebella
motto: "Perseverance is the key to success."
description: When she was quite young, the family moved from there to Nelson county, N. D., where she received her early education at a country school. Her home for several years has been in Grand Forks. She took the preparatory course at the "U," and then entered the present class.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Hyslop, Christine
motto: "Truth is strengthened by her touch."
description: She believes that men are, at best, poor weak creatures, and that it is the duty of women, as Christians, to guide and protect them.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_08.png

name: Ego, C. S.
description: Mr. Ego, from Lisbon, N. D., has by his record fully demonstrated that he will become one of the foremost criminal lawyers of this country. Ego scorns the petty squabbles of the college political world. He has marked sympathy for the pedestrians of the Turtle Mountains, and yet he is anxious to have it understood that he does not hail from that part of the country.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Skulason, Skuli G.
description: Born in Canada, educated in the United States. Took preparatory and college courses at the U. N. D. Was prominent in debate, oratory and society, while taking academic course. He has announced his intention of becoming at least a United States Congressman.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Duggan, F. S.
description: Mr. Duggan was born and raised in Grand Forks, N. D., a graduate of our State University. He is universally recognized as the good-looking man of our class, and the only ladies' man among us. He has, however, decided not to marry, for he is an eminently fair-minded man and to make a choice, he holds, would require a showing of partiality which he scorns. He will spend the major portion of his time in establishing the fact that he is correct, when he does not agree with the weight of authority, for Duggan has the courage of his convictions.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Johnson, J. G.
description: Mr. Johnson comes from Milton, N. D. We have picked him out as a specialist in all legal matters pertaining to railroads. He has already earned fame in moot court as an aggressive trial lawyer. We expect great things of Johnson, for, if he can keep from falling in love, he will make his mark in the world.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_09.png

name: Nesbitt, D. W.
description: Moved with his folks to Stonewall, Texas, in 1875, and in 1885 immigrated to Northern Wyoming, residing there until coming to North Dakota in 1896. STATE OF NORTH DAKOTA, COUNTY OF GRAND FORKS—SS. D. W. Nesbitt, being first duly sworn, deposes and says, that the above is a true autobiography, except as to matters therein stated on information and belief, and as to those he believes it to be true.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_10.png

name: McLennan, D. J.
description: Mr. McLennan, the plucky little Scotchman, hails from the town of Marshall, Minn., having taken his first year of law in the U. of Minn. He is very fleet of foot, but, true to the spirit and reputation of his race, refuses to run from any man though twice his size, except at track meets—so says our honored Secretary, Andrew A. Bruce.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Scramstad, Martin
description: We must place him in the same class as Wright, Bascom and Hall for Cupid overpowered him all of a sudden last year, and he was married forthwith. We admire him for his peaceful disposition and ability to take things as they come without any excitement or worry. He seldom smiles and never laughs, never talks except when it is necessary, and moves with a mathematical precision.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Messersmith, J. E.
description: He is a former University boy. Having spent one year at the U. of Minn., he returned to us to finish his legal education. With him he brought a native of Breckenridge, whose photo appears farther on. The firm of Smith & Messersmith has already gained considerable notoriety, and we are confident that their fame will grow as the years roll by.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_10.png

name: Bergman, H. A.
description: We have almost forgiven him the sin he committed, when he strayed out of his own state for his preliminary education. We like him, but we would have known him better and liked him more had he graduated from the U. N. D. Bergman is afraid of girls, which he would not be had he graduated from our own "U." Moral: Take your education in your own state in a co-educational institution.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Haraldson, E. O.
description: For many reasons he is our favorite. On the football team he is a tower of strength. Many a tenderfoot has thanked his lucky stars for a place of safety, and hid behind him, when a fight seemed imminent on the gridiron. If ever you get into trouble, go to Haraldson; for he is the best natured man who ever lived, and his schemes and resources are as innumerable as the girls he has on the brain.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Wright, C. D.
motto: "He is the only man that Bascom cannot overrule on points of parliamentary law."
description: Mr. Wright, our honored class president, comes from Wynmere, N. D. Mr. Wright takes great delight in being quizzed, especially in Criminal Law. He has shown marked ability as a politician. As one of the married men of the class he has our sympathy. He is our patriarch, our wise man and guide in times of class trouble. He often gets tired of our boyish pranks and summarily adjourns class meetings.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Smith, E. A.
description: As stated above, this specimen of the genus homo comes from the town of Breckenridge, Minn. Smith does not believe in moving unless he absolutely has to. He makes haste slowly, but is always there in time, a very good way. When pitching he often waits the batter out instead of the batter waiting him out. He is one of our handsome boys, as the cut shows.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_11.png

name: Bascom, H. T.
major: Law School of Highland Park, Iowa
description: Mr. Bascom is a graduate of the Law School of Highland Park, Iowa, a member of the firm of Hartzell & Bascom, of Langdon, N. D. Though he is married we count him one of the boys, a jolly good fellow. His highest ambition is to re-cast all existing parliamentary rules, and evolve a complete set of his own.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Hartzell, H. L.
description: Mr. Hartzell, of Langdon, N. D., is a graduate of Highland Park, Iowa. He was originally captured in the lowlands of Abyssinia and brought to this country. Since then there has been a complete transformation in him. He is now one of our brightest and best behaved students. There is so little left now of the matted hair which the picture shows and which formerly gave him a wild appearance, that we may well apply the principle, "De minimis non curat lex."
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Hall, J. B.
description: Mr. Hall comes to us from Milford, Iowa, where he spent his early school days. He, like Bascom, Scramstad and Wright, is a "Benedict," but we like him all the same, for he mingles with us "youngsters," in spite of his added responsibilities. He has a great aim in life, namely, to make the Iowa Code the World Code. At that he will work with ceaseless energy and indomitable courage.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Coghlan, M. J.
description: Mr. Coghlan comes from our wild western country in the vicinity of St. John, where the savage Red Men still roam the prairie. This genial son of Erin is a favorite among the Red Men, and it is rumored that he is getting tired here in our midst and yearns for the free west, the tepee and all the native pleasures of a pioneer country. We will keep him as long as we can, for he is good company.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Parsons, C. M.
description: Mr. Parsons comes from Lidgerwood, N. D. We call him "Judge" because of his fairness, impartiality, and sedate, dignified bearing. About the hardest problem he has solved is what to buy for Christmas presents. We have decided to put him on the Supreme Bench, being certain that there will still at that time be some mooted questions for his clear mind to elucidate.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_12.png

name: Frederick, H. Larson
motto: "Venus gave her son a wealth of comely locks."
description: Mr. Larson is the champion of three victorious inter-society debates and one victorious inter-collegiate debate. He is the most enthusiastic member of Adelphi, besides being a partner in the University Bookstore Firm and taking an active part in all "U" interests.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Josephine, Nelson
motto: "An effort made for the benefit of others lifts us above ourselves."
description: This young lady spent her freshman and sophomore years at the Red River Valley "U," but seeing her opportunity to join our class, she entered the University of North Dakota last fall. Her home is now in Grand Forks.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Tessie, E. Jackson
motto: "Like streams that keep a summer wind snow-hid in January."
description: This popular young lady came to us from "Erin" by way of Langdon, N. D. She is a prominent writer of Celtic verse, and an authority on the Celtic language. She is a person of marked scholarship and is very liberal minded.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Ella, M. Robertson
motto: "Grace was in all her steps, heaven in her eye, in every gesture, dignity and love."
clubs: Editor of Societies and Organizations of Dacotah
description: Miss Robertson is a highly esteemed member of this immortal class. She is a young lady of ability and scholarship. She is a faithful worker both in class and in the Adelphi.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Lee, Lane Wilcox
motto: "Tender and True."
description: Lee was born in New York state, but came west to North Dakota to grow up with the country. Graduated from the Larimore High School in spring of '99, with highest honors. Entered the "U" in the fall of '99, and became a member of the famous football team of that year. Began the arts course, but changed in third year to Sophomore Mining Engineering class. He also acts as student assistant in Physics.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_13.png

name: Stevens, J. Floyd
motto: "The world was sad, the garden was a wild, and man, the hermit, sighed, till woman smiled."
description: This popular young man represents the thriving town of Larimore. Wherever he is known, he is known as a hearty friend and an entertaining companion.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_14.png

name: Coulter, John L.
motto: "He is most excellent who can suit his temper to any circumstances."
description: Because of his unbounded enthusiasm, his oratorical ability and his famous joke book, Mr. Coulter is a prominent member of literary and social circles. He is known to his classmates and fellow Adelphians as a tireless and self-sacrificing worker.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_14.png

name: Rysstad, Olaf H.
motto: "Beauty draws without a single hair."
description: Mr. Rysstad comes from Soetersdalen, Norway. He attended school at Fisher, and later entered the "U" of Minnesota in the fall of 1899. He has taught several terms of school in both Minnesota and North Dakota. He is a near relative of Gunar Rysstad, a rising young Norwegian poet.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_14.png

name: Duell, Earl L.
motto: "She could not understand."
description: A young man who has already won fame as the editor of that mighty political organ and leader of advanced thought, "Starkweather Times." He resigned the editorial chair in order to continue his studies here.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_14.png

name: Byrne, Mathew
motto: "So wise, yet so young."
description: Distinguished for not belonging to any society and also distinguished for his great learning. He is a very bright and studious young man of whom his classmates are not a little proud.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_14.png

name: Conmy, Lucy B.
motto: "A perfect woman, nobly planned. To warn, to comfort and command."
description: Miss Conmy is one of the most prominent young ladies of the University. She is an excellent student and in all her work is diligent, efficient and unselfish. She takes an active part in literary work, and is also an enthusiastic tennis player.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_15.png

name: Devaney, Henry J.
motto: "Hank"
description: "Hank" took his preparatory course at the U. N. D., and then stayed out two years in order to join the class of '04. His sturdy Irish integrity and gentlemanly demeanor won for him the position of station agent. Mr. Devaney is a conscientious worker but in spite of his studious nature, he occasionally has a "Lulu" of a time.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_15.png

name: Ward, Innis W.
motto: "All who joy would win, must share it. Happiness was born a twin."
description: Mr. Ward's home is in Farmington, N. D. He entered the "U" as a preparatory student in the fall of 1898. He is an excellent student and athlete; and is the very personification of good-fellowship.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_15.png

name: Sprague, Gilbert M.
motto: "Behold a man of solid worth."
description: Mr. Sprague is of a serious and practical turn of mind. He taught school several months since he began his college course, but has not found it necessary to fall behind his class. He has avowed his intention of graduating with his hair at least an inch longer than that of any other gentleman in his class.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_15.png

name: Rockne, Ludvig M.
motto: "The proper study of mankind is man; the most perplexing one, no doubt, is woman."
description: Mr. Rockne is a young man of excellent character, and was selected to represent the Y. M. C. A. of the University at Lake Geneva last year. He has elected a Cottage course for his Junior year and, apparently, his heart is in his work.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_16.png

name: Grimson, Gudmunder
motto: "See'st thou a man diligent in his business."
description: Mr. Grimson came to the "U" in the fall of 1898 from Milton, N. D. He has been on the winning team of three inter-society debates, and won the Thomas Gold Medal for improvement in debate in 1900. He is postmaster at the University; chief promoter of the Dacotah; and mail carrier from the "Cottage."
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_16.png

name: Abbey, R. Percy
motto: "He has wit, words and worth, action, utterance and the powers of speech."
description: Mr. Abbey's home is in Langdon, N. D. He entered the Preparatory Department in the fall of 1899. He is a young man of artistic nature, and is distinguished for his oratorical ability and ready wit. He has formed the habit of getting his name on the honor roll and of being last to appear in the dining hall for breakfast.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_16.png

name: Haugen, Martin O.
motto: "Domestic Happiness! thou only bliss of Paradise that has survived the fall."
description: Mr. Haugen entered the Preparatory Department of the University in the fall of 1896. He is a good student, thorough and persevering in his work. He has had a thrilling hairbreadth escape from being a very handsome man.
image: 页面提取自－University of North Dakota_1904.pdf_页面_16.png
